items.title: 'I've got all the symptoms': Matty Robbins was a hero of the AFL. Now he's suffering

children.content: More than 30 professional and amateur Aussie Rules players have been diagnosed with CTE, revealing that the brain trauma crisis is striking all levels of the game.

articles.title: They didn't know what footy was doing to their boy. 'Now it's too late'

children.content: A talent and a team player, Nick Lowden excelled at Aussie rules. But the knocks, bumps, hits and collisions were adding up.

items.title: Quick hits: Canada's emotional match-winner and South Korea's team blasted

children.content: Canada's heroes tread new ground, South Korea's extreme reaction to going out and scenes in Algeria's home away from home. Here's the five quick hits from the World Cup.

children.content: The Northern Territory Police Force has moved to reassure Territorians that new, armed public safety officers due to hit the beat from today are fully trained to carry guns.

items.title: Ageing 'Nashos' call for priority as all veterans get equal access to health benefits

children.content: All Australian veterans will soon have access to claim compensation for health issues, fulfilling a royal commission recommendation, but those conscripted during the Vietnam War say their advanced age means their claims should receive priority.

items.title: Qld government dumps plan to turn gold mine into pumped hydro

items.title: 'Big loss': Doctors call for fatigue policy review after car crash death

children.content: Doctors are calling for Tasmania's Department of Health to examine its fatigue management policies after a doctor died on the state's roads. Artyom Avetisyan, a cardiothoracic surgery registrar at the Royal Hobart Hospital, has been remembered by hospital staff as a kind and compassionate colleague who will be "greatly missed".

items.title: Telstra phasing out CAN Radio phone system amid customer safety concerns

children.content: Telstra's Customer Access Network Radio has been a lifeline for many for more than 40 years but its technology has reached the end of its life. Customers now fear a new system will not be as reliable in an emergency.
